original_abstract: The rapid maturation of deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) has catalyzed transformative breakthroughs across the computer vision landscape, particularly in image classification, semantic segmentation, object detection, and super-resolution. Distinct from traditional machine learning, CNNs excel at autonomous feature engineering, learning hierarchical representations directly from raw input data through specialized architectural configurations. As deep learning techniques have evolved, CNN architectures have grown increasingly sophisticated and diverse, effectively superseding conventional methods. This paper provides a fundamental analysis of core CNN components—including convolutional and pooling layers, activation functions, batch normalization, and regularization techniques like dropout—explaining their specific roles within the network. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive review of the historical development and current state-of-the-art applications of CNNs in fields such as object detection and video prediction. Finally, we synthesize the primary challenges facing deep CNNs, survey existing solutions, and deliberate on promising directions for future research.
humanized_abstract: The accelerated development of deep Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) has seen incredible improvements across the realm of computer vision. Image classification, semantic segmentation, object detection and super-resolution have seen advancements that greatly surpass the traditional machine learning approaches. Unlike other approaches that require a deep understanding of the features and hence require hand-engineered features, the deep learning networks based on CNNs are able to automatically learn very deep features of the input data. These features are learnable by the virtue of their deep architecture design. Today, CNNs have far surpassed the conventional methods. This paper serves to act as a building block of knowledge that introduces the basic components of CNN which include convolutional, pooling layers, activation functions, batch normalization and the regularization techniques like dropout. Apart from this, it presents the development and status of the applications of CNNs in object detection and video prediction and finally gives the summative overview of the main challenges faced by deep CNNs, current approaches and future research work in this direction.
